behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the routing algorithm itself (heuristic quality bound), not external I/O. A deterministic in-process test can feed coordinate sets, run the heuristic, compute/lookup optimal lengths, and assert ratio <= 1.5. Rejected higher levels: behavior text focuses on "heuristic produces a route" and does not require real HTTP, OS/subprocess, or AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic module directly with a corpus of fixed coordinate instances (including edge layouts) and compare route length against precomputed optimal lengths for those instances; assert each ratio is <= 1.5 for cases representing up to 20-stop inputs.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses module boundaries: stop-to-stop distance data plus road-type speed configuration plus estimator logic. A pure unit test of only formula math can miss wiring bugs (wrong config key, wrong road-type mapping, wrong unit conversion across modules). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no AI dependency, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real estimator service with in-process distance provider and config repository implementations (or realistic fakes), run routes containing mixed road types, and assert produced time windows match expected durations from distance/speed per segment and accumulated schedule.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Failure is local decision logic: compare total estimated time vs remaining shift and compute excess. No required cross-module handoff, real infrastructure, or nondeterministic model output. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is explicitly validator logic ("rejects... returning the excess amount").
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the validator function with table-driven cases (under limit, equal limit, over limit, boundary rounding) and assert rejection flag plus exact excess-time value when over limit.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff: geocoding result handling -> filtering invalid stop -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting. A unit test of just one function would miss end-to-end local orchestration bugs. Rejected System: behavior specifies logical API outcome "returns no results"; this can be reproduced with an in-process stub response, so real network is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service orchestration with a stub geocoding client returning normal coordinates for most addresses and empty results for one address; assert optimized route excludes failed address, remaining addresses are still optimized, and skipped-address list contains exactly the failed input.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans deduplication/aggregation logic and downstream route construction/visit list. The bug often appears when preprocessing output is consumed by optimizer (duplicate still visited, or items not merged). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed, no AI/ML dependency, no multi-step agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the route-building pipeline with repeated addresses carrying different items; assert output stop list has one consolidated stop per duplicate key, combined items are preserved, and final route visits that location exactly once.
